2606.26922.title: Risk-Aware Selective Multimodal Driver Monitoring with Driver-State World Modeling
2606.26922.paper_summary: Continuous driver monitoring in automated vehicles requires low-latency inference while avoiding unsafe decisions under uncertain driver states. Large vision-language models provide broad multimodal priors, but their latency and limited reliability in this setting make them unsuitable as always-on in-cabin monitors. We propose a cost-aware selective inference framework for deployable multimodal driver monitoring. The core system is a lightweight RGB-physiological student that combines in-cabin visual observations with window-level HR/EDA signals, and a learned gate that decides when to accept the fast prediction or abstain for safety intervention. Additional controls show that the learned scores contain sample-level information beyond scenario priors, while exact physiological synchronization remains a limitation. To incorporate predictive evidence, we further study a compact driver-state world modeling module that rolls out latent driver-state features and estimates future fast-model errors and counterfactual system-level action costs. On scenario-induced driver-demand recognition, the RGB-physiological student improves over RGB-only and physiology-only baselines, reaching 0.7440 Macro-F1 and 0.9099 balanced accuracy with 11.39M parameters and 3.08ms inference latency. Cost-aware selective inference reduces unsafe false negatives from 17.37% under always-fast inference to approximately 5% across seeds, while maintaining deployment-level latency. While driver-state world modeling offers valuable predictive signals, worst-group evaluations highlight persistent operating-point calibration drift. Ultimately, reliable edge driver monitoring requires advancing not only perception backbones, but also risk-aware selective control and group-robust calibration.
2606.26922.paper_summary_zh: 持續的自動駕駛車輛駕駛員監控需要低延遲的推斷，同時避免在不確定的駕駛員狀態下做出不安全的決策。大型視覺-語言模型提供了廣泛的多模態先驗，但它們在這種情境下的延遲和有限的可靠性使其不適合作為始終開啟的車艙監控器。我們提出了一個成本感知的選擇性推斷框架，用於可部署的多模態駕駛員監控。核心系統是一個輕量級的RGB-生理學生，它將車艙內的視覺觀察與窗口級的心率/EDA信號相結合，並且有一個學習的閘門決定何時接受快速預測或為安全干預而放棄。額外的控制顯示，學習的分數包含超越情境先驗的樣本級信息，而精確的生理同步仍然是一個限制。為了納入預測證據，我們進一步研究了一個緊湊的駕駛員狀態世界建模模塊，該模塊推出潛在的駕駛員狀態特徵並估計未來的快速模型誤差和反事實系統級行動成本。在情境引起的駕駛員需求識別上，RGB-生理學生在RGB僅和生理僅基準上有所改進，達到0.7440的Macro-F1和0.9099的平衡準確率，擁有11.39M參數和3.08ms的推斷延遲。成本感知的選擇性推斷將不安全的假陰性從始終快速推斷下的17.37%降低到大約5%，同時保持部署級延遲。雖然駕駛員狀態世界建模提供了有價值的預測信號，但最差組評估突顯了持續的操作點校準漂移。最終，可靠的邊緣駕駛員監控需要不僅提升感知骨幹，還要進步風險感知的選擇性控制和群體穩健校準。
2606.26922.author: Daosheng Qiu et.al.
2606.26922.authors: Daosheng Qiu, Haozhuang Chi, Hao Su, Shu Long, Xinyue Miao, Yongle Dong, Wei Zhang
